meaning: n.通行税(费),费,代价,钟声vt.征收,敲钟,鸣(钟)(特指宣布死亡),勾引,引诱vi.征税,鸣钟
examples: The official death toll has now reached 7 000.
There are fears that the casualty toll may be higher.
Most people who drive the toll roads don't use them every day.
Why would the presence of a silent cell phone inflict such a heavy toll?
Horton increased 2.4%, PulteGroup 0.1%, Lennar 0.7%, KB Home 1%, and Toll Brothers 0.1%.
